title: trifolium arvense subsp. phleoides
intro: Trifolium arvense subsp. phleoides, also known as the field clover or phleoides subclover, is a subspecies of the wild clover plant that is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Trifolium arvense subsp. phleoides include its ability to reduce blood sugar levels, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a supplement in capsule or powder form, although it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
